title: Using Monday to share campaign toolkit to the public
excerpt: Hi there, I’d like to use Monday to create a campaign toolkit like you can using trello (see this as the example) The board comes straight from the landing webpage and can easily be viewed on a phone and desktop/laptop. …

posts.author: Fionam (Fiona Meighan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, I’d like to use Monday to create a campaign toolkit like you can using trello (see this as the example) The board comes straight from the landing webpage and can easily be viewed on a phone and desktop/laptop. Is it possible to do the same thing in Monday? We’ve just started using Monday at my work so I’d rather stay with it if it does what we need and is easy for members of the public to access and use (view only). Any help or hints would be very much appreciated. Thank you.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d recommend creating a Dashboard (Blank), then you can add widgets (bookmarks, embed, etc)

posts.author: rehan.abdul (Rehan AQ | monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Fiona, welcome to community!

You can definetly do that, On the board create a blank view and then you can make it shareable view. Here is the easy guide for you.

Regards
Rehan AQ

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Fiona! I hope you’re doing well. Thanks for sharing the Trello example and walking us through your use case. Echoing Rehan, you can share your board view via a shareable link. Anyone accessing the link cannot edit the board view or your board. They will only have access to view the data displayed in the board view.

If you’re looking to mirror a similar structure to Trello, you can add a Kanban View to your board and share that view!
